summary: This cluster focuses on the biotechnological exploitation of marine microalgae for the production of high-value bioactive compounds. Research centered on *Nannochloropsis oculata* and *Dunaliella salina* investigated their sterol and carotenoid biosynthesis pathways, respectively, to identify and enhance compounds with therapeutic potential. Key findings included the anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer activities of a sterol-rich fraction from *N. oculata*, while studies on *D. salina* leveraged genomic and metabolic engineering to improve its stress tolerance and isoprenoid production. The geographic scope was primarily laboratory-based, with an emphasis on cultured strains and genetic manipulation rather than field collection.
